raw: {"weights":{"R1.1":0.7,"R1.2":1.0,"R1.3":0.7,"R1.4":0.3,"R1.5":0.7,"R1.6":0.7,"R1.7":0.3,"RX.1":1.0,"RX.2":1.0,"RX.3":1.0,"RX.4":1.0,"RX.5":1.0},"active_set":["R1.1","R1.2","R1.3","R1.5","R1.6","RX.1","RX.2","RX.3","RX.4","RX.5"],"category_focus":"R1","clarification_needed":false,"clarification_question":"","rationale":{"R1.1":"A section review is expected to assess whether the thesis and contribution framing cohere, especially because the artifact shifts from DialogueDev to RSP as the central contribution.","R1.2":"Checking the internal argument chain is load-bearing because the section makes a causal/framing move from multi-agent protocol design to prompting-level gains.","R1.3":"The review should assess whether the reported benchmark numbers and configuration comparisons support the claims made from them.","R1.5":"Section-level narrative organization is central because the passage appears to introduce one system and then reframe the contribution around another mechanism.","R1.6":"A general section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.","RX.1":"The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended by the short delegation.","RX.2":"The review responsibility should avoid endorsing claims beyond what the excerpt itself supports.","RX.3":"The responsibility remains limited to reviewing the provided section rather than executing revisions or evaluating the whole paper.","RX.4":"Potential downstream harms include misleading contribution framing or unsupported performance claims in a research artifact.","RX.5":"Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation and the specific claims present in the excerpt."}}

rationale.R1.1: A section review is expected to assess whether the thesis and contribution framing cohere, especially because the artifact shifts from DialogueDev to RSP as the central contribution.
rationale.R1.2: Checking the internal argument chain is load-bearing because the section makes a causal/framing move from multi-agent protocol design to prompting-level gains.
rationale.R1.3: The review should assess whether the reported benchmark numbers and configuration comparisons support the claims made from them.
rationale.R1.5: Section-level narrative organization is central because the passage appears to introduce one system and then reframe the contribution around another mechanism.
rationale.R1.6: A general section review normally includes sentence- and paragraph-level clarity, flow, register, and concision.
rationale.RX.1: The projection must preserve uncertainty about what kind of review is intended by the short delegation.
rationale.RX.2: The review responsibility should avoid endorsing claims beyond what the excerpt itself supports.
rationale.RX.3: The responsibility remains limited to reviewing the provided section rather than executing revisions or evaluating the whole paper.
rationale.RX.4: Potential downstream harms include misleading contribution framing or unsupported performance claims in a research artifact.
rationale.RX.5: Any projected responsibility should remain traceable to the delegation and the specific claims present in the excerpt.
